summary: Moved the Warp Star Jump portion of ft_0C31 out of assembly into a new ftCommon C translation unit, reaching 99.97% file match across 1.15 KB. The PR added C implementations or guarded ASM fallbacks for ftCo_800C4724, ftCo_WarpStarJump_Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll/Cam, helper callbacks ftCo_800C4858 and ftCo_800C48AC, and the transition helper ftCo_800C4A38. It also added the build object, a dedicated ftCo_WarpStarJump.h header, Warp Star Jump motion variables, needed item/animation prototypes, and corrected ftCo_DatAttrs.x14C from int to float to match its use in it_80294430.
what_changed: Removed 331 lines of Warp Star Jump assembly and related literals from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s while leaving later Warp Star Fall assembly in that file.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_WarpStarJump.c with 460 lines covering Warp Star Jump entry, animation, physics, collision, camera, damage/death callbacks, and the transition into ftCo_MS_WarpStarFall.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_WarpStarJump.h and moved Warp Star Jump public prototypes out of src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h.
Added the new C object to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_0C31.s object.
Included ftCommon/ftCo_WarpStarJump.h from ftCo_Init.c and ftCo_ItemGet.c so existing users resolve against the new module header.
Added ftCommon motion-variable layout for warpstarjump: cur_pos at fp+2340, self_vel at fp+234C, facing_dir at fp+2358, x1C timer at fp+235C, and x20 at fp+2360.
Updated ftCo_DatAttrs.x14C from int to float, matching its use as the first float argument to it_80294430.
Expanded it/items/itwstar.h with prototypes for it_80294364, it_80294430, it_802944AC, and it_802946B0, plus address comments for existing declarations.
The PR report says all listed functions were 100% except ftCo_800C4724 at 99.87%, producing a 99.97% file score.
smart_moves: Isolated this action-state family into a dedicated ftCommon source/header instead of keeping new declarations in the broad ft_0C31.h catch-all.
Kept local static helpers for non-public callbacks and transition logic, while exposing only the action callbacks needed by other files.
Used a named motion-vars struct for fp+2340 onward, turning repeated raw offsets into meaningful Warp Star Jump state fields.
Captured the shared damage/death callback behavior in inlineA0, showing that ftCo_800C4858 and ftCo_800C48AC perform the same high-level restore/update sequence.
Preserved matchability with ASM functions guarded by !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP), allowing readable C while retaining exact assembly fallback where needed.
Pulled item and animation function prototypes into their owning headers instead of relying on placeholder or undeclared calls.
Promoted constants from assembly sdata/sdata2 into typed C literals, including 0, 1, M_PI_2, and the assert strings "jobj.h" and "jobj".
decomp_lessons: When splitting a chunk out of a monolithic asm file, add the new C object immediately before the remaining asm object to preserve link order for nearby symbols and literals.
Action-state decomp often requires both code and data-layout work: here the C only became readable after adding the warpstarjump member to ftCommon_MotionVars.
Callback assignments can reveal static helper boundaries: fp->take_dmg_cb and fp->death2_cb pointed at local labels that became ftCo_800C4858 and ftCo_800C48AC.
Use callsite types to correct struct fields; x14C had to be a float because it is loaded as a float and passed to it_80294430 with another float.
Item-action interactions should be typed in item-specific headers, not left as generic UNK calls, once the fighter code depends on them.
Small do-nothing callbacks such as IASA and Coll can be represented as empty C functions when the assembly is just blr.
Camera wrappers can often match directly as a one-call function; ftCo_WarpStarJump_Cam simply calls ftCamera_800762F4.
naming_conventions: New module follows ftCommon action-file naming: ftCo_WarpStarJump.c and ftCo_WarpStarJump.h.
Public action callbacks use the action-state suffix pattern ftCo_WarpStarJump_Anim, _IASA, _Phys, _Coll, and _Cam.
Still-unknown or internal functions retained address-based names such as ftCo_800C4724, ftCo_800C4858, ftCo_800C48AC, and ftCo_800C4A38.
Motion-variable member is named warpstarjump inside union ftCommon_MotionVars, with partially known fields named cur_pos, self_vel, facing_dir, x1C, and x20.
Header include guard for the new header uses the address-style GALE01_0C4724 pattern.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted removed labels .L_800C4858 and .L_800C48AC into static C functions and referenced them directly in callback fields.
Used ASM void functions with nofralloc blocks under the matching branch, while keeping C bodies under the non-MUST_MATCH/WIP branch.
Used #pragma peephole on after embedded ASM blocks, consistent with local matching style.
Used literal globals with the original symbol names ftCo_804D8D48, ftCo_804D8D4C, ftCo_804D8D50, ftCo_804D3C58, and ftCo_804D3C60 to replace assembly sdata/sdata2.
Mapped repeated stack Vec initialization from assembly to Vec3 pos with pos.x = pos.y = pos.z = 0 in the shared callback inline.
Matched physics by reading JObj translation and storing frame-to-frame deltas into fp->self_vel while caching the latest translation in mv.co.warpstarjump.self_vel.
Matched the fall transition by preserving the order of item calls, motion-state change, flag/callback setup, blast-zone positioning, JObj rotation, item cleanup, and mpColl_80043680 update.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching ftCo_800C4724 from 99.87% to 100%.
Search for later renames of ftCo_800C4858, ftCo_800C48AC, and ftCo_800C4A38.
Check whether warpstarjump.x1C and x20 received semantic names in later decomp work.
Look for follow-up PRs that decompile ftCo_WarpStarFall functions still left in ft_0C31.s.
Audit other users of ftCo_DatAttrs.x14C to confirm the float correction propagated safely.
